info.name: IPBuilding Gateway
info.description:
# IPBuilding Gateway — REST API (v0.4.0)

Open field-bus gateway for IPBuilding controllers (relay / dimmer / input modules on UDP/1001). This collection exercises the product northbound REST API exposed by the gateway add-on on port `8080`.

## Base URL

```
{{gateway_host}}:{{gateway_port}}/api/{{apiVersion}}
```

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `base_url` | `http://homeassistant.local:8080` | Gateway host (HA add-on: `http://[HOST]:[PORT:8080]`) |
| `apiVersion` | `v1` | API version — currently only `v1` is shipped |
| `module_id` | `00:24:77:52:ac:be` | Stable MAC-based module identifier (lower-case, colon-separated) |
| `device_id` | `10.10.1.30-0` | Logical channel ID — `{module_ip}-{channel}` or a custom slug |

## Device-ID format

Logical channel IDs use the form `{module_ip}-{channel}` (e.g. `10.10.1.30-0` for the first channel of the relay at `10.10.1.30`). A custom slug from `devices.json` (e.g. `keuken-led`) is also accepted wherever `{device_id}` is used.

## Device-type resolution (server-side only)

The gateway **resolves** the device type (`relay` / `dimmer` / `input`) from the installation config (`devices.json`) on every request. Clients never supply a device type and cannot spoof it: sending `{"action": "DIM"}` to a relay `device_id` yields `422 unsupported device type: relay`, not a `DIM` payload on the field bus.

## Response shapes

**Success envelope** (command results, forced discovery):

```json
{"ok": true}
```

**Resource envelopes** wrap the payload in a named key:

```json
{"modules": [...]}
{"devices": [...]}
```

**Error envelope** (command results):

```json
{"ok": false, "error": "<reason>"}
```

**Not-found envelope** (resource lookups):

```json
{"error": "not found"}
```

## HTTP status code conventions

| Code | Meaning | Example |
|------|---------|---------|
| `200` | OK | Command accepted; resource returned |
| `400` | Missing or malformed input | `{"ok": false, "error": "missing 'action'"}` |
| `404` | Unknown entity | `{"error": "not found"}` (module_id or device_id not in registry) |
| `422` | Unsupported action / device-type mismatch | `{"ok": false, "error": "unsupported relay action: FOO"}` |
| `501` | Endpoint reserved, not yet implemented | `{"ok": false, "error": "not yet implemented"}` (Fase 8) |

## See also

- [`docs/api/rest.md`](https://github.com/markminnoye/IPBuilding-Gateway/blob/main/docs/api/rest.md) — full REST reference
- [`docs/api/websocket.md`](https://github.com/markminnoye/IPBuilding-Gateway/blob/main/docs/api/websocket.md) — WebSocket `/ws` message catalog (real-time state push + command)
- [`ARCHITECTURE.md`](https://github.com/markminnoye/IPBuilding-Gateway/blob/main/ARCHITECTURE.md) — northbound protocol design

variable.description: API path segment. v1 is the only version currently served by the gateway.
Hostname of the gateway. Override per-environment via Postman environment variables (recommended) or edit this default.
HTTP port of the gateway northbound API. Default 8080.
Pre-filled device id for the Commands folder. Format: {module_ip}-{channel}. Edit or override via environment if your installation uses different addresses.

item.name: Modules
item.description: Physical field-bus controllers. Each module is identified by its stable MAC address (`module_id`) and exposes cached `getSysSet` network metadata plus, for input modules, `getButtons` button configuration. Modules are looked up server-side; the gateway never trusts IP-based identity (DHCP can change IPs at any time). Runtime-only fields `last_seen` and `last_seen_source` are populated by the passive ARP monitor — see [`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-04-runtime-auto-discovery-design.md`](https://github.com/markminnoye/IPBuilding-Gateway/blob/main/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-04-runtime-auto-discovery-design.md) for the write policy.

request.description:
Return every physical field-bus module with cached network metadata. The list is built from the in-memory `InstallationConfig` (loaded from `devices.json`) merged with the latest HTTP `getSysSet` / `getButtons` data from each module.

**Path:** `GET /api/{{apiVersion}}/modules`

**Response 200 — modules array** (one entry per physical controller):

```json
{
  "modules": [
    {
      "id": "00:24:77:52:ac:be",
      "ip": "10.10.1.30",
      "name": "IP0200PoE",
      "model": "IP0200PoE",
      "type": "relay",
      "firmware": "5.1",
      "mac": "00:24:77:52:ac:be",
      "network": {
        "dhcp": "0",
        "ip": "10.10.1.30",
        "subnet": "255.255.255.0",
        "gateway": "10.10.1.1"
      },
      "button": "0",
      "allow": "",
      "fetched_at": "2026-06-04T18:00:00Z",
      "last_seen": "2026-06-04T20:15:00Z",
      "last_seen_source": "arp"
    }
  ]
}
```

`last_seen` and `last_seen_source` are **runtime-only** — never persisted to `devices.json`. They are filled by the passive ARP monitor (`source: "arp"`), HTTP `getSysSet` calls (`source: "http"`), or UDP activity on port 1001 (`source: "udp"`). When the cache has never seen the module, both fields are absent.

See [`docs/api/rest.md`](https://github.com/markminnoye/IPBuilding-Gateway/blob/main/docs/api/rest.md) for the full field reference and [`docs/api/websocket.md`](https://github.com/markminnoye/IPBuilding-Gateway/blob/main/docs/api/websocket.md) for real-time updates over `/ws`.

Return a single module by its MAC-based `module_id` (lower-case, colon-separated). The lookup is case-insensitive — the gateway normalises the MAC before comparison, so `00:24:77:52:AC:BE` and `00:24:77:52:ac:be` resolve to the same record.

**Path:** `GET /api/{{apiVersion}}/modules/{module_id}`

**Path parameters:**

| Name | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `module_id` | string | Normalised MAC address, e.g. `00:24:77:52:ac:be` |

**Response 200** — single module object, same shape as the entries in the list endpoint. Runtime `last_seen` and `last_seen_source` are included when the ARP monitor has observed the module.

**Response 404** — `{"error": "not found"}` when the MAC is not in the installation config.

See [`docs/api/rest.md`](https://github.com/markminnoye/IPBuilding-Gateway/blob/main/docs/api/rest.md).

Force a synchronous refresh of the module metadata cache. The gateway will re-fetch `getSysSet` from every module in the installation and `getButtons` from input modules. The HTTP timeout per module is `2.0 s` by default.

**Path:** `POST /api/{{apiVersion}}/modules/refresh`

**Request body:** empty JSON object `{}` (no parameters). The Content-Type header is required by aiohttp's JSON parser.

**Response 200** — same envelope as `GET /api/v1/modules`, with `fetched_at` timestamps advanced to the moment of refresh and `last_seen` / `last_seen_source` reflecting the most recent observation.

Use this endpoint after on-device reconfiguration (DHCP, subnet, button mapping) to surface the changes immediately. The companion (`ipbuilding-gateway-ha`) refreshes the cache on its own cycle; manual calls are useful for debugging or one-off tests.

See [`docs/api/rest.md`](https://github.com/markminnoye/IPBuilding-Gateway/blob/main/docs/api/rest.md).

item.name: Devices
item.description: Logical channels (lights, fans, switches) that map onto physical modules. Each device is identified by a `device_id` in the form `{module_ip}-{channel}` (e.g. `10.10.1.30-0`) or by a custom slug from `devices.json`. Devices carry the channel's current state — relay `on`/`off` or dimmer `level` — as reported by the in-memory `DeviceRegistry`.

request.description:
Return every logical device (active channels only) with its current runtime state.

**Path:** `GET /api/{{apiVersion}}/devices`

**Response 200** — devices array, joined with the parent module's MAC and current IP:

```json
{
  "devices": [
    {
      "id": "10.10.1.30-0",
      "module_id": "00:24:77:52:ac:be",
      "module_ip": "10.10.1.30",
      "channel": 0,
      "name": "Keuken LED",
      "room": "Keuken",
      "semantic_type": "light",
      "device_type": "relay",
      "active": true,
      "max_watt": 60,
      "state": "off",
      "current_watt": 0
    },
    {
      "id": "10.10.1.40-0",
      "module_id": "00:24:77:52:9e:a8",
      "module_ip": "10.10.1.40",
      "channel": 0,
      "name": "Woonkamer Dimmer 1",
      "room": "Woonkamer",
      "semantic_type": "light",
      "device_type": "dimmer",
      "active": true,
      "max_watt": 200,
      "state": "on",
      "level": 75,
      "current_watt": 150
    }
  ]
}
```

Inactive channels (`active: false` in `devices.json`) are filtered out. Dimmer entries include `level` (0–100) and a derived `current_watt` (`max_watt * level // 100`); relay entries include a binary `current_watt` (`max_watt` when on, else `0`).

See [`docs/api/rest.md`](https://github.com/markminnoye/IPBuilding-Gateway/blob/main/docs/api/rest.md) for the field reference and [`docs/api/websocket.md`](https://github.com/markminnoye/IPBuilding-Gateway/blob/main/docs/api/websocket.md) for the `snapshot` and `state_changed` push messages.

Return a single device (logical channel) by its `device_id`. The ID is either `{module_ip}-{channel}` (e.g. `10.10.1.30-0`) or a custom slug from `devices.json` (e.g. `keuken-led`).

**Path:** `GET /api/{{apiVersion}}/devices/{device_id}`

**Path parameters:**

| Name | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `device_id` | string | `{module_ip}-{channel}` or a configured slug |

**Response 200** — single device object, same shape as the entries in the list endpoint. Includes the most recent state from the in-memory `DeviceRegistry` (relay: `state` + `current_watt`; dimmer: `level` + derived `current_watt`).

**Response 404** — `{"error": "not found"}` when the device_id is not in the installation config.

See [`docs/api/rest.md`](https://github.com/markminnoye/IPBuilding-Gateway/blob/main/docs/api/rest.md).

item.name: Commands
item.description:
Send actions to relay or dimmer channels. The gateway resolves the `device_id` to its physical module + channel and then encodes the right field-bus command. Device type is determined server-side from `devices.json` — clients cannot spoof it.

| Action | Valid for | Extra fields | Status on success |
|--------|-----------|--------------|-------------------|
| `ON` | relay | — | `200 {"ok": true}` |
| `OFF` | relay | — | `200 {"ok": true}` |
| `PULSE` | relay | — | `200 {"ok": true}` |
| `TOGGLE` | relay | — | `200 {"ok": true}` |
| `DIM` | dimmer | `value` (0–100, `0` = off) | `200 {"ok": true}` |

request.description:
Switch the relay channel identified by `{{default_device_id}}` ON.

**Path:** `POST /api/{{apiVersion}}/devices/{device_id}/command`

**Request body:**

```json
{"action": "ON"}
```

**Path parameters:**

| Name | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `device_id` | string | `{module_ip}-{channel}` or a configured slug, e.g. `10.10.1.30-0` |

**Response 200** — `{"ok": true}`. The relay should report its new state over the WebSocket `state_changed` message within the next poll cycle.

**Response 400** — `{"ok": false, "error": "missing 'action'"}` when the body lacks an `action` field.

**Response 422** — `{"ok": false, "error": "unsupported relay action: FOO"}` when the action verb is not one of `ON` / `OFF` / `PULSE` / `TOGGLE` for a relay channel.

See [`docs/api/rest.md`](https://github.com/markminnoye/IPBuilding-Gateway/blob/main/docs/api/rest.md).

Switch the relay channel identified by `{{default_device_id}}` OFF.

**Path:** `POST /api/{{apiVersion}}/devices/{device_id}/command`

**Request body:**

```json
{"action": "OFF"}
```

**Response 200** — `{"ok": true}`.

**Response 400** — `{"ok": false, "error": "missing 'action'"}`.

**Response 422** — `{"ok": false, "error": "unsupported relay action: FOO"}` for unknown verbs.

See [`docs/api/rest.md`](https://github.com/markminnoye/IPBuilding-Gateway/blob/main/docs/api/rest.md).

Send a short PULSE to the relay channel identified by `{{default_device_id}}`. Pulse duration is fixed by the IP0200PoE firmware (typically 1 s ON / 1 s OFF).

**Path:** `POST /api/{{apiVersion}}/devices/{device_id}/command`

**Request body:**

```json
{"action": "PULSE"}
```

**Response 200** — `{"ok": true}`.

**Response 400** — `{"ok": false, "error": "missing 'action'"}`.

**Response 422** — `{"ok": false, "error": "unsupported relay action: FOO"}` for unknown verbs.

See [`docs/api/rest.md`](https://github.com/markminnoye/IPBuilding-Gateway/blob/main/docs/api/rest.md).

Flip the relay channel identified by `{{default_device_id}}` (ON → OFF, OFF → ON). The gateway reads the latest known state from the `DeviceRegistry` to decide which way to flip; if the state is unknown, the firmware default of "switch ON" is sent.

**Path:** `POST /api/{{apiVersion}}/devices/{device_id}/command`

**Request body:**

```json
{"action": "TOGGLE"}
```

**Response 200** — `{"ok": true}`.

**Response 400** — `{"ok": false, "error": "missing 'action'"}`.

**Response 422** — `{"ok": false, "error": "unsupported relay action: FOO"}` for unknown verbs.

See [`docs/api/rest.md`](https://github.com/markminnoye/IPBuilding-Gateway/blob/main/docs/api/rest.md).

Set the dimmer channel identified by `{{default_device_id}}` to a specific level. The `value` field is an integer percentage between `0` and `100` (`0` = OFF, encoded as a special "dim off" payload rather than a 0% level).

**Path:** `POST /api/{{apiVersion}}/devices/{device_id}/command`

**Request body:**

```json
{"action": "DIM", "value": 75}
```

**Response 200** — `{"ok": true}`. The dimmer confirms the new level on the next status reply (tracked under the correct channel via the gateway's dimmer channel tracker).

**Response 422** — `{"ok": false, "error": "unsupported dimmer action: FOO"}` when the action is not `DIM`, or `{"ok": false, "error": "unsupported device type: input"}` when the device_id resolves to a non-dimmer channel (relay / input).

**Response 400** — `{"ok": false, "error": "missing 'action'"}`.

See [`docs/api/rest.md`](https://github.com/markminnoye/IPBuilding-Gateway/blob/main/docs/api/rest.md).

item.name: Status
item.description: Gateway health and operator status. Aggregate status uses ok/degraded/unhealthy with per-issue warnings. The HA companion polls GET /api/v1/status and receives WebSocket gateway_status pushes when status or open issues change.

request.description: Return the full gateway health snapshot: aggregate status (ok/degraded/unhealthy), version, uptime, subsystem health, open issues with user-facing messages, and links to operator actions (discover sweep, module metadata refresh). Used by the ipbuilding-gateway-ha companion for sw_version and the gateway status sensor.

item.name: Discovery
item.description:
Runtime auto-discovery. The gateway normally uses a passive ARP monitor to track new, changed, and removed modules on the field bus. This folder exposes a single synchronous trigger that forces a full sweep on demand — useful from operator dashboards, scripted provisioning flows, or after physical reconfiguration. The forced sweep ignores the `passive_arp_monitor` and `auto_discover_on_start` toggles and is always available.

See [`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-04-runtime-auto-discovery-design.md`](https://github.com/markminnoye/IPBuilding-Gateway/blob/main/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-04-runtime-auto-discovery-design.md) for the full write policy and design rationale.

request.description:
Trigger a synchronous ARP-first + HTTP identify sweep of the field bus. The endpoint blocks until the sweep completes (typically 5–30 s, depending on `discovery_range_end` and `http_timeout_s`). The endpoint is always available regardless of the `passive_arp_monitor` and `auto_discover_on_start` toggles.

**Path:** `POST /api/{{apiVersion}}/discover`

**Request body:** none. The body is optional — the gateway accepts an empty body or `{}`. (Mark the request in Postman as having no body; do not send a JSON object unless you want to log a parser warning.)

**Response 200** — discovery summary:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "added": ["00:24:77:52:ac:be"],
  "changed": [],
  "removed": [],
  "duration_ms": 2341
}
```

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `ok` | boolean | `true` if the sweep completed (even when no changes were detected) |
| `added` | array of MAC strings | Modules newly discovered — written to `devices.json` with `active: false, room: "Unconfigured"` |
| `changed` | array of MAC strings | Modules whose firmware version or IP changed (IP changes update the runtime registry only) |
| `removed` | array of MAC strings | Modules not seen for the configured threshold — runtime marker only, **not** removed from `devices.json` |
| `duration_ms` | integer | Total sweep time in milliseconds |

A `discovery_completed` event with the same payload is also broadcast on the WebSocket `/ws` channel for any connected clients (companion, dashboards).

See [`docs/api/rest.md`](https://github.com/markminnoye/IPBuilding-Gateway/blob/main/docs/api/rest.md) and the runtime auto-discovery design spec linked above.

item.name: Provisioning
item.description: Operator-driven provisioning flows. The gateway owns a small set of actions that push persistent state to module EEPROM (autonomy tables, button mapping). These endpoints are wired up in the router but most are scheduled for Fase 8 of the implementation roadmap; the autonomy endpoint is currently a stub that returns `501 not yet implemented` so the contract is locked in.

request.description:
Reserved endpoint for syncing the `autonomy` (input button → action) table to an IP1100PoE module's EEPROM via the `saveAutonomy` HTTP call. The route is wired and the response contract is locked, but the implementation lands in Fase 8. Today the endpoint always returns `501 not yet implemented` so that clients can already be coded against the final shape.

**Path:** `POST /api/{{apiVersion}}/provision/autonomy`

**Request body:** `{}` (reserved; the body is accepted but ignored by the stub).

**Response 501** — `{"ok": false, "error": "not yet implemented"}`.

See [`docs/api/rest.md`](https://github.com/markminnoye/IPBuilding-Gateway/blob/main/docs/api/rest.md).
